title_ar: تفسير الخازن — الربع الأخير من القرآن
content_id:
Popularitas abadi Tafsir al-Khazin di seluruh dunia Islam Arab dari abad keempat belas hingga hari ini merupakan fenomena signifikan dalam sejarah kesalehan dan beasiswa Islam. Meskipun mendapat kritik ilmiah yang serius tentang metodologi hadisnya, karya ini terus dibaca, dicetak, dan dikonsultasikan oleh umat Islam yang mencari kombinasi antara komentar ilmiah dan pengayaan devotional.

Tradisi kritis hadis mengidentifikasi masalah-masalah yang signifikan dengan riwayat-riwayat dalam Tafsir al-Khazin. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, ulama hadis terbesar abad kelima belas, dan ulama-ulama kemudian mencatat kehadiran tradisi-tradisi lemah dan palsu, khususnya dalam bagian-bagian fada'il (keutamaan) untuk surah-surah individual. Banyak dari tradisi-tradisi ini — yang menjanjikan pahala-pahala spesifik untuk melafalkan surah-surah tertentu — ditelusuri oleh kritikus-kritikus kemudian kepada perawi-perawi yang diketahui telah memalsukan hadis. Kritik ini telah diulang oleh ulama-ulama kontemporer yang merekomendasikan kehati-hatian ketika mengutip riwayat-riwayat yang dilaporkan al-Khazin.

Meskipun ada konsensus kritis ini, Tafsir al-Khazin dicetak ulang di seluruh dunia Arab dan tetap menjadi perabot di rumah-rumah dan masjid-masjid. Sebagian daya tariknya justru karena kekayaan naratif dan kombinasi konten ilmiah dan devotionalnya — kualitas-kualitas yang membuatnya cocok bagi pembaca yang mencari keterlibatan dengan al-Quran sebagai teks spiritual yang hidup daripada sebagai objek analisis akademis.

Di Afrika Utara, Mesir, dan Levant khususnya, Tafsir al-Khazin adalah di antara karya tafsir yang paling banyak dimiliki dan dibaca oleh umat Islam terpelajar biasa yang bukan ulama terlatih. Pengaruhnya pada kesalehan Islam populer di kawasan-kawasan ini — melalui akun-akun naratifnya tentang para nabi, deskripsi-deskripsinya tentang surga dan neraka, dan perlakuan motivasionalnya terhadap perintah-perintah al-Quran — telah substansial bahkan ketika para ulama memperingatkan agar tidak mengutip riwayat-riwayatnya tanpa kritis.

Bagi sejarawan agama Islam populer, Tafsir al-Khazin menyediakan jendela yang tak ternilai ke dalam budaya devotional dunia Islam abad pertengahan dan awal modern — menunjukkan jenis narasi, motivasi, dan pengayaan spiritual yang dicari oleh pembaca yang luas dalam keterlibatan mereka dengan al-Quran.
content_en:
Tafsir al-Khazin's enduring popularity across the Arab Islamic world from the fourteenth century to the present day represents a significant phenomenon in the history of Islamic piety and scholarship. Despite serious scholarly criticism of its hadith methodology, the work has continued to be read, printed, and consulted by Muslims seeking a combination of scholarly commentary and devotional enrichment.

The hadith critical tradition identified significant problems with the narrations in Tafsir al-Khazin. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, the greatest hadith scholar of the fifteenth century, and later scholars noted the presence of weak and fabricated traditions, particularly in the fada'il (virtues) sections for individual surahs. Many of these traditions — promising specific rewards for reciting specific surahs — were traced by later critics to narrators known to have fabricated hadith. This criticism has been repeated by contemporary scholars who recommend caution when citing al-Khazin's reported narrations.

Despite this critical consensus, Tafsir al-Khazin was reprinted throughout the Arab world and remained a fixture in homes and mosques. Part of its appeal was precisely its narrative richness and its combination of scholarly and devotional content — qualities that made it suitable for readers who sought engagement with the Quran as a living spiritual text rather than as an object of academic analysis.

In North Africa, Egypt, and the Levant particularly, Tafsir al-Khazin was among the most widely owned and read tafsir works for ordinary educated Muslims who were not trained scholars. Its influence on popular Islamic piety in these regions — through its narrative accounts of the prophets, its descriptions of paradise and hell, and its motivational treatment of Quranic commands — has been substantial even when scholars warned against citing its narrations uncritically.

For historians of Islamic popular religion, Tafsir al-Khazin provides an invaluable window into the devotional culture of the medieval and early modern Islamic world — showing what kinds of narrative, motivation, and spiritual enrichment a wide readership sought in their engagement with the Quran.
content_ar: التفسير al-Khazin's enduring popularity across the Arab Islamic الدنيا from the fourteenth century to the present day represents a المهم phenomenon in the history of Islamic piety and scholarship. رغم serious العلمي criticism of its الحديث methodology, the work has continued to be read, printed, and consulted by المسلمون seeking a combination of العلمي commentary and devotional enrichment. The الحديث البالغ الأهمية التراث identified المهم problems with the narrations in التفسير al-Khazin. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, the greatest الحديث scholar of the fifteenth century, and later العلماء noted the presence of weak and fabricated traditions, particularly in the fada'il (الفضائل) sections for individual السور. Many of these traditions — promising specific rewards for reciting specific السور — were traced by later critics to narrators known to have fabricated الحديث. This criticism has been repeated by contemporary العلماء who recommend caution عندما citing al-Khazin's reported narrations. رغم this البالغ الأهمية consensus, التفسير al-Khazin was reprinted throughout the Arab الدنيا and remained a fixture in homes and mosques. Part of its appeal was precisely its narrative richness and its combination of العلمي and devotional content — qualities that made it suitable for readers who sought engagement with the القرآن الكريم as a living spiritual text rather than as an object of الأكاديمي analysis. In North Africa, Egypt, and the Levant particularly, التفسير al-Khazin was among the most widely owned and read التفسير works for ordinary educated المسلمون who were not trained العلماء. Its influence on popular Islamic piety in these regions — through its narrative accounts of the الأنبياء, its descriptions of الجنة and النار, and its motivational treatment of القرآني commands — has been substantial even عندما العلماء warned against citing its narrations uncritically. For historians of Islamic popular religion, التفسير al-Khazin provides an invaluable window into the devotional culture of the medieval and early modern Islamic الدنيا — showing what kinds of narrative, motivation, and spiritual enrichment a wide readership sought in their engagement with the القرآن الكريم.
